Aya Rashid is a Cloud Operations Architect with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, LinkedIn, and a personal website.

Aya Rashid completed a Post Graduate Diploma in Information Technology from Symbiosis Institute of Computer Studies & Research in January 2013 in Pune, India, and a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Telecommunications from Savitribai Phule Pune University in January 2007, also in Pune, India.

From January 2007 to March 2009, the individual worked as a Senior System Engineer at Patni Computer Systems Limited in Pune, India, for Genworth Financial, focusing on maintaining MySQL replication across Linux servers for data redundancy and high availability, and fine-tuning applications for performance.

The individual provided support to L1 engineers, troubleshooting complex system issues, and monitored the P8 Filenet application to ensure optimal performance and uptime while working on the CPI project for Genworth Imaging Solutions.

The individual managed and resolved user-raised tickets, administered 10 Linux Proxy servers and MySQL servers, implemented MySQL replication, prepared SQL queries for Oracle database operations, and tested queries before production deployment.

The individual managed bugs, implemented MySQL replication on Linux servers, performed database backups using mysqldump, ensured functionality of customer applications like eProcess and Trexo, and customized Filenet instances.

The individual wrote shell scripts to automate tasks, including deleting logs older than five days, which was successfully deployed to production, and utilized platforms like P8 Filenet, Linux/Unix, and IBM AIX, along with software like HTML and shell scripting.

From January 2007 to March 2009, the individual maintained and enhanced applications for Genworth Imaging Solutions, managed data storage in Oracle Database and P8 Filenet, and developed reports for providers.

From 2007 to the present, the individual developed numerous shell scripts and AWS Lambda functions to automate operational tasks, including AWS instance shutdown and log deletion, enhancing system administration efficiency, reliability, and security.
The candidate possesses skills in Python for 6 months, SQL, and HTML.
The candidate has experience with databases including MySQL, Oracle, and DynamoDB.
The candidate is familiar with operating systems such as Linux/Unix, IBM AIX, and Windows.
The candidate has experience with other technologies including P8 Filenet, Tibco Spotfire, RHDS, CVS, VDI, and Appdynamics.
The candidate is proficient in methodologies such as Automation for 72 months, Solution Architecture, System Administration, Production Support, Team Leadership, and Recruitment.

The individual possesses skills in cloud platforms like AWS and Azure, DevOps tools such as Puppet and Redhat Satellite Server, and scripting languages like Shell Scripting with 96 months of experience.

From April 2015 to the present, Aya Rashid worked as a Cloud Operations Architect (DevOps) at Egy-Tech Cloud Solutions in Cairo, Egypt, where she managed Tibco Spotfire, built a team of 15, worked on multiple projects, coordinated deployments, led critical projects, organized AWS workshops, and developed shell scripts for automation and cost optimization.

From April 2012 to March 2015, Aya Rashid was an Administrator, Automation & Monitoring Specialist at Synechron Technologies Limited in Alexandria, Egypt, where she managed Linux servers, performed patching, created Satellite channels, implemented automation solutions, and provided database support.

From April 2009 to March 2012, Aya Rashid was a Developer, Automation & Monitoring Specialist at Tsys Limited in Cairo, Egypt, where she managed user access, handled database operations, and planned environment setup.

March 2009 marked the close of a nearly three-year chapter for this engineer, a period deeply rooted in the intricate world of data management and system stability. He arrived at Patni Computer Systems Limited in Pune, India, back in January 2007, immediately dedicating himself to the critical task of ensuring Genworth Financial’s data remained secure and accessible. He wasn’t building flashy new systems, but rather the silent guardian of existing ones – a meticulous architect of redundancy. His expertise lay in the delicate art of MySQL replication across Linux servers, a constant vigil against data loss, ensuring high availability even under pressure. 

Beyond simply *keeping* things running, he possessed a knack for optimization. He wasn’t content with functionality; he sought performance, subtly tuning applications to run leaner and faster. This dedication extended to Genworth Imaging Solutions, where he wasn’t just maintaining applications, but actively enhancing them. He navigated the complexities of Oracle Database and P8 Filenet, managing the flow of information and ensuring its organized storage. He understood the need for clarity, translating raw data into actionable insights through the creation of reports for providers, a bridge between technical infrastructure and practical application. March 2009 wasn’t an ending, but a point of culmination – a solid foundation of experience built on reliability, performance, and a quiet mastery of complex systems.

Shell scripts are Aya Rashid’s quietly powerful tools – the digital workhorses she’s been crafting and deploying for over fifteen years. Born from a need to streamline and secure operational tasks, these aren’t merely lines of code, but automated solutions honed through experience on platforms ranging from the robust foundations of Linux/Unix and IBM AIX to the dynamic landscape of AWS. Initially focused on practical necessities like purging aged logs – a five-day lifespan being a common benchmark – they quickly evolved into sophisticated systems for managing AWS instances, optimizing costs, and bolstering overall system reliability. 

Rashid’s scripts aren’t theoretical exercises; they’ve consistently found their way into production environments, proving their worth under real-world pressure.  Her expertise extends beyond simple automation, encompassing integration with tools like Tibco Spotfire and a deep understanding of deployment coordination. They represent a core component of her approach to Cloud Operations, a testament to her ability to translate complex needs into elegant, efficient, and impactful solutions.  They are the silent engine driving efficiency, security, and scalability within the systems she manages.

A seasoned architect of digital infrastructure, this individual embodies the quiet power of System Administration. For over fifteen years – since 2007 – they’ve been less a responder *to* system issues and more a proactive builder of resilient, automated environments. Think of them as a digital custodian, meticulously crafting solutions that anticipate and prevent disruption. 

Their expertise isn’t simply in *maintaining* systems, but in fundamentally *improving* them. A master of scripting – fluent in shell and increasingly, serverless AWS Lambda functions – they’ve consistently translated manual, repetitive tasks into elegant, automated workflows. This isn’t about replacing people, but freeing them; their work on automating instance shutdown and log deletion speaks to a dedication to both efficiency and security. 

Beyond the technical prowess, lies a strategic mind. They aren’t just implementing solutions, they’re designing them – a true Solution Architect. This extends to a broader understanding of Methodologies, encompassing not just *how* things are done, but *why*.  A natural leader, they’ve also demonstrated the ability to build and nurture high-performing teams, extending their influence beyond individual code to encompass Recruitment and Production Support, ensuring long-term stability and growth.  They don’t just fix problems; they build systems that require fewer of them, and empower others to do the same.

P8 Filenet is a robust, enterprise-level content management system, a digital archive at the heart of Genworth Imaging Solutions’ operations. It’s a workhorse, reliably storing and managing vast quantities of data – alongside Oracle Database – and demanding consistent attention to maintain optimal performance and uptime. Not a glamorous tool, perhaps, but essential. Those who work with it aren’t simply users; they’re custodians, proactively monitoring its health and building automated solutions to streamline its function. Scripts, often crafted in shell, are its lifeblood, keeping it lean and efficient – like the automated deletion of aged logs, a practical improvement successfully implemented in production. While comfortable alongside more modern platforms like Tibco Spotfire and Appdynamics, P8 Filenet remains a foundational element, a testament to enduring technology, and a key component in a broader technological ecosystem encompassing Linux/Unix, IBM AIX, and even the practical utility of HTML. It’s a system that rewards diligence and a knack for problem-solving, a silent partner in the delivery of critical reports and the smooth functioning of a complex imaging infrastructure.

Linux is the quiet workhorse underpinning critical systems, a robust and versatile operating system favored by those who demand reliability and control. It’s not flashy, but deeply dependable – the foundation upon which data integrity and performance are built. Think of it as the seasoned engineer, content to operate behind the scenes, ensuring everything runs smoothly. 

For Genworth Financial, through the hands of a Senior System Engineer in Pune, India (2007-2009), Linux manifested as a guardian of vital data. It diligently maintained the mirroring of MySQL databases across multiple servers, a constant, silent replication ensuring no information was lost and systems remained available even under pressure. This wasn’t about innovation, but about unwavering stability – a bedrock for financial operations.

Later, in Alexandria, Egypt, with Aya Rashid at Synechron Technologies (2012-2015), Linux took on a more proactive role. It became a platform for automation, a canvas for streamlining server management through patching and the creation of custom distribution channels (Satellite). Here, Linux wasn’t just *supporting* systems, it was being *shaped* to improve them, a testament to its adaptability. It also provided the necessary environment for database support, seamlessly integrating with the data infrastructure. 

Its strength lies in this duality: a steadfast base for essential functions like MySQL replication – guaranteeing data redundancy – and a flexible platform for advanced administration and automation. Linux isn’t a single entity, but a powerful environment, consistently leveraged to ensure systems remain resilient, efficient, and ultimately, trustworthy.

January 2007 marked the beginning of a focused period for this engineer, a time deeply rooted in the practicalities of data integrity and system performance. For nearly two and a half years, they served as a Senior System Engineer at Patni Computer Systems Limited, embedded within the Genworth Financial project in Pune, India. They were a guardian of data flow, meticulously maintaining MySQL replication across a network of Linux servers – a silent architect of redundancy and unwavering availability. Beyond simply *keeping* things running, they possessed a keen eye for optimization, constantly fine-tuning applications to squeeze out every ounce of performance. 

Their responsibilities extended beyond the core database infrastructure. They were also a caretaker of Genworth Imaging Solutions’ applications, nurturing and improving them over time. A comfortable hand with both Oracle Database and P8 Filenet, they managed the complex landscape of data storage, ensuring its accessibility and security. Finally, they translated raw data into actionable insights, crafting reports specifically tailored for providers, bridging the gap between technical systems and practical business needs. This period defined them as a pragmatic, detail-oriented engineer, comfortable navigating the complexities of large-scale data management and committed to delivering reliable, high-performing solutions.

Aya Rashid is a seasoned Cloud Operations Architect based in Cairo, Egypt, a specialist in building and optimizing cloud infrastructure. Her career, rooted in a strong technical foundation – a Bachelor’s in Electronics and Telecommunications from Savitribai Phule Pune University and a Post Graduate Diploma in IT from Symbiosis Institute – has steadily progressed from automation and monitoring to leading complex cloud initiatives. 

For nearly a decade at Egy-Tech Cloud Solutions, Aya has been a driving force, not only managing critical tools like Tibco Spotfire but also cultivating a high-performing team of fifteen. She’s a hands-on leader, coordinating deployments, spearheading critical projects, and sharing her expertise through AWS workshops. A pragmatic problem-solver, Aya consistently leverages shell scripting to automate processes and drive cost optimization.

Her earlier roles at Synechron Technologies and Tsys Limited honed her skills in system administration, database support (particularly MySQL), and automation. From managing Linux servers and implementing patching strategies to creating Red Hat Satellite channels and scripting solutions for user access across platforms like RHDS, CVS, and DOT Project Servers, Aya has consistently demonstrated a talent for streamlining operations and ensuring system reliability. She’s readily accessible via email (aya.rashid@email.com), phone (+20 100 123 4567), and maintains an active presence online through her GitHub (github.com/aya-rashid), LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/aya-rashid), and personal website (ayarashid.com).

A quiet efficiency defines this individual’s work. Not a creator of grand designs, but the meticulous hand ensuring everything *runs*. They are a resolver, a troubleshooter who calmly navigates the complexities of user issues, bringing order to chaos one ticket at a time. Beneath that calm lies a deep technical proficiency – a fluency in the language of servers. Ten Linux and MySQL servers fall under their care, kept humming with consistent performance, even bolstered by the implementation of MySQL replication for enhanced reliability. 

They aren’t afraid to get their hands dirty with the raw power of SQL, crafting precise queries for Oracle, then rigorously testing them before unleashing them into the live environment. Automation is a key principle; tedious tasks are tamed with elegantly written shell scripts. One such script, designed to prune old logs, now quietly maintains system health in production, a testament to their proactive approach. 

Their toolkit is diverse – P8 Filenet, the robust stability of Linux/Unix and IBM AIX, even the foundational building blocks of HTML and shell scripting. They aren’t simply *using* these tools, but weaving them together to build a stable, responsive, and ultimately, reliable production environment. The success of their shell scripts, directly deployed to production, speaks to a careful, considered approach – a dedication to solutions that work, and work well. They are the unseen architect of smooth operation, the guardian of uptime.

Aya Rashid is a seasoned Cloud Operations Architect, a builder and leader deeply entrenched in the world of cloud infrastructure. For nearly a decade – since April 2015 – she’s been a pivotal force at Egy-Tech Cloud Solutions in Cairo, Egypt, shaping their cloud strategy and execution. Rashid isn’t simply *familiar* with AWS; she actively *propagates* its understanding, regularly organizing and leading AWS workshops, effectively growing expertise within and beyond her organization. 

Her skillset extends beyond the platform itself, encompassing the broader DevOps ecosystem – she’s proficient with Azure, Puppet, and Redhat Satellite Server – and grounded in practical automation through robust shell scripting. This isn’t theoretical knowledge; Rashid has demonstrably applied these skills to real-world challenges, developing scripts specifically for cost optimization and streamlining deployments. 

A natural leader, she built and mentored a team of fifteen, consistently taking the helm on critical projects and coordinating complex deployments. Rashid’s role isn’t just about maintaining systems; it’s about proactive improvement, strategic management of tools like Tibco Spotfire, and a commitment to efficiency and innovation within the cloud environment. She’s a hands-on architect, a dedicated mentor, and a driving force in leveraging AWS to deliver tangible business value.

HTML is a foundational language of the web, a quiet architect shaping the visible world of online experience. Not flashy, but utterly essential. It’s a skill readily paired with more dynamic languages like Python – a burgeoning talent for this individual, honed over six months – and a comfortable companion to the structured logic of SQL. While not a standalone power, HTML provides the skeletal structure, the very bones upon which digital content is built. This individual understands its importance, leveraging it alongside robust systems administration skills – a familiarity with Linux/Unix, IBM AIX, and platforms like P8 Filenet – and the practical efficiency of shell scripting, used to automate critical tasks like log management. It’s a core competency, a building block in a broader skillset geared towards building and maintaining functional, accessible digital spaces. It’s a language of presentation, and in the hands of someone who also commands scripting and database skills, a powerful tool for delivering information.
